wire_delay_model.links_note: 8x8 axial hex projected onto same 20mm x 20mm die / 2.5 mm tile pitch. Cardinal links (4 of 6 neighbours) = 2.5 mm = 1 cycle. Diagonal links (2 of 6 neighbours, axis +i/-j) = 2.5 * sqrt(2) ~ 3.536 mm = 2 cycles (wire delay 318 ps > 250 ps period). Generator: /tmp/hexa-arch-rfc001-measurement/runs/gen_topologies.py.

verdict.rationale: single-topology record; F1/F2 verdict requires the pair-verdict aggregator over this + the degree-4 mesh mate at identical (traffic, node, wire_delay_model)

wire_delay_source.node_basis: SMART 22nm TSMC GP, characterization Table 2; reaffirmed OpenSMART ISPASS 2017 §III

scope_caveats: single (clock, node, placement) point — not a regime claim
axial-hex on square grid same-sign pair diameter = 14 (same as d=4 mesh); degree-6 advantage only helps opposite-sign pairs — alternative hex placements (e.g. king-move d=8) unevaluated
4 GHz is the first integer-cycle frequency where diagonal links cost 2 cycles; clock sweep not performed
22nm SMART is the most-modern public characterization; newer-FinFET sensitivity not performed
routing = min (DOR variant); adaptive routing under-utilises diagonal links and may understate d=6 throughput
